chapters.name: Physical World and Measurement
chapters.description: Introduction to physics, scope and excitement of physics, nature of physical laws. Units and measurements including SI units, dimensional analysis.

chapters.name: Kinematics
chapters.description: Motion in straight line and plane, projectile and circular motion. Position, displacement, velocity, acceleration concepts.

chapters.name: Laws of Motion
chapters.description: Newton's laws of motion, inertia, momentum, impulse, conservation of momentum. Force, equilibrium, friction.

chapters.name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
chapters.description: Center of mass, momentum conservation, rigid body rotation, moment of inertia, torque, angular momentum.

chapters.name: Gravitation

chapters.name: Properties of Bulk Matter

chapters.name: Thermodynamics

questions.text: A body of mass 2 kg is moving with velocity 10 m/s. What is its kinetic energy?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Kinetic Energy = (1/2)mv² = (1/2)(2)(10)² = 100 J

questions.text: Which law states that an object at rest stays at rest unless acted upon by an external force?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Newton's First Law, also known as the law of inertia, states that an object at rest stays at rest and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

questions.text: The dimensional formula of force is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Force = mass × acceleration. [F] = [M][LT⁻²] = MLT⁻²

questions.text: A projectile is fired at an angle of 45° with horizontal. What is the range if initial velocity is 20 m/s? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Range R = (u²sin2θ)/g = (20²×sin90°)/10 = 400/10 = 40 m

questions.text: The escape velocity from Earth's surface is approximately:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The escape velocity from Earth is approximately 11.2 km/s, calculated using v_e = √(2gR) where R is Earth's radius.

questions.text: What is the charge on a proton?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: A proton carries a positive charge of +1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ coulombs, which is the fundamental unit of charge.

questions.text: Ohm's law states that V = IR. If resistance is doubled and current is halved, what happens to voltage?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: V = IR. New V = (I/2)(2R) = IR. Therefore voltage remains the same.

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic field is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The SI unit of magnetic field (B) is Tesla (T), named after Nikola Tesla. 1 Tesla = 1 Weber/m².

questions.text: Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction relates to:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Faraday's law states that induced EMF is equal to the negative rate of change of magnetic flux through a coil.

questions.text: The refractive index of water is approximately:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The refractive index of water is approximately 1.33, meaning light travels 1.33 times slower in water than in vacuum.

questions.text: What is the atomic number of carbon?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Carbon has atomic number 6, meaning it has 6 protons in its nucleus.

questions.text: The number of moles in22.4 L of any gas at STP is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: At STP (Standard Temperature and Pressure), 22.4 L of any ideal gas contains 1 mole of gas molecules (Avogadro's law).

questions.text: Which of the following has maximum ionization energy?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ionization energy decreases down a group. Li is at the top of Group 1, so it has the highest ionization energy among these elements.

questions.text: The shape of NH₃ molecule is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: NH₃ has one lone pair and three bond pairs. According to VSEPR theory, this gives a trigonal pyramidal shape.

questions.text: Which gas law states PV = nRT?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The ideal gas equation PV = nRT combines Boyle's, Charles', and Avogadro's laws.

questions.text: Hess's law is based on:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Hess's law states that total enthalpy change is independent of path, based on conservation of energy.

questions.text: pH of pure water at 25°C is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Pure water at 25°C has pH = 7, which is neutral. [H⁺] = [OH⁻] = 10⁻⁷ M.

questions.text: The oxidation state of Cr in K₂Cr₂O₇ is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In K₂Cr₂O₇: 2(+1) + 2x + 7(-2) = 0, solving gives x = +6.

questions.text: Which is the lightest halogen?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Fluorine (F) is the lightest halogen with atomic mass of approximately 19 amu.

questions.text: Colligative properties depend on:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Colligative properties (osmotic pressure, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, vapor pressure lowering) depend only on the number (concentration) of solute particles, not their nature.

questions.text: If A = {1, 2, 3} and B = {2, 3, 4}, then A ∩ B is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: Intersection A ∩ B contains elements common to both sets: {2, 3}.

questions.text: The value of sin²θ + cos²θ is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: This is the fundamental trigonometric identity: sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 for all values of θ.

questions.text: The modulus of complex number 3 + 4i is:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: The number of ways to arrange 5 people in a row is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: Number of arrangements = 5! = 5 × 4 × 3 × 2 × 1 = 120

questions.text: In binomial expansion of (x + y)ⁿ, the number of terms is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: The binomial expansion (x + y)ⁿ has (n + 1) terms.

questions.text: The sum of first n natural numbers is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: Sum = n(n+1)/2. This is the formula for sum of arithmetic progression 1, 2, 3, ... n.

questions.text: The slope of line perpendicular to y = 2x + 3 is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: If slope of line is m, perpendicular line has slope -1/m. Here m = 2, so perpendicular slope = -1/2.

questions.text: The eccentricity of a circle is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: A circle has eccentricity e = 0, as it has no elongation.

questions.text: The derivative of x² is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: Using power rule: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹. So d/dx(x²) = 2x.

questions.text: If A is a 2×2 matrix with det(A) = 5, then det(2A) is:
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.explanation: For n×n matrix, det(kA) = kⁿ×det(A). Here n=2, so det(2A) = 2² × 5 = 20.
